category_notes.Bills: Recurring/utility invoices: ISP, phone, electricity. Telkom (telkomsa.net) maps here.
category_notes.Finance: Bank statements, payment processors, crypto wallets. FNB + ABSA in ALL CAPS to match existing label style. Stripe + Wirex use title case.
category_notes.Newsletters: Recurring mailing-list / digest content. Sender has List-Unsubscribe header or is a known newsletter platform.
category_notes.Notifications: Event/activity notices: login alerts ('login from...', 'new sign-in'), purchase confirmations ('purchase for $X'), account-access warnings ('someone from X is accessing your account'), trade confirmations ('your trade for $X succeeded'). Distinct from Receipts: notifications are about account/security activity; receipts are commerce documents.
category_notes.Personal: Mail from individual humans, typically via gmail.com / hotmail.com / icloud.com.
category_notes.Receipts: Order confirmations, invoices, payment receipts from commerce. Subject patterns: 'receipt', 'invoice', 'order confirmation', 'your order'.
category_notes.Shopping: E-commerce promotional + order tracking from stores. iStore = Apple Store SA.
category_notes.Work: Work-related senders: VPM team (vpmteam.co.za), Busyweb (busyweb.co.za = own work domain).

edits_from_proposal: Added new top-level 'Bills' for utility/ISP mail (Telkom).
Dropped 'Other' bucket entirely — unmatched messages stay unlabeled (we'll review at end of Phase 3).
Dropped Receipts/Telkom — those messages get Bills/Telkom in Phase 3 (and can additionally get Receipts via multi-label).
Notifications kept at top level; sub-labels remain Apple/Google plus Phase 3 will route transactional patterns here.
Existing 55 labels left untouched (option A); nested labels are additive.

labeling_policy.approach: Adopt nested tree fresh. Existing flat labels (ABSA, FNB, Medium, LinkedIn, Nedbank, Amazon, etc.) are NOT migrated in this session. Phase 3 applies new nested labels to matching messages; old labels keep their existing message set untouched. Migration is a separate session.
